heading: Denial of transfer of missile equipment or technology by United States persons
label: 2797a Denial of transfer of missile equipment or technology by United States persons

children.label: A
children.text: exports, transfers, or otherwise engages in the trade of any item on the MTCR Annex, in violation of the provisions of , section 4604 or 4605 of title 50 or any regulations or orders issued under any such provisions,section 2778 of this title1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: conspires to or attempts to engage in such export, transfer, or trade, or

children.label: C
children.text: facilitates such export, transfer, or trade by any other person,

children.label: i
children.text: United States Government contracts relating to missile equipment or technology; and

children.label: ii
children.text: licenses for the transfer of missile equipment or technology controlled under this chapter.

children.label: i
children.text: all United States Government contracts, and

children.label: ii
children.text: all export licenses and agreements for items on the United States Munitions List.

children.heading: Discretionary sanctions
children.label: b Discretionary sanctions
children.text: In the case of any determination made pursuant to subsection (a), the President may pursue any penalty provided in .section 2778(c) of this title

children.heading: Presumption
children.label: c Presumption
children.text: In determining whether to apply sanctions under subsection (a) to a United States person involved in the export, transfer, or trade of an item on the MTCR Annex, it should be a rebuttable presumption that such item is designed for use in a missile listed in the MTCR Annex if the President determines that the final destination of the item is a country the government of which the Secretary of State has determined, for purposes of 4605(j)(1)(A) of title 50, has repeatedly provided support for acts of international terrorism.2So in original. Probably should be followed by “section”.21
the product or service is essential to the national security of the United States; and

such person is a sole source supplier of the product or service, the product or service is not available from any alternative reliable supplier, and the need for the product or service cannot be met in a timely manner by improved manufacturing processes or technological developments.
